rawReport:
CT CHEST ENHANCED
Patient Name: segmed_LASTNAME, segmed_FIRSTNAME
            Unit No: segmed_ACC_NUM
 
 
EXAM#     TYPE/EXAM                       
segmed_MRN_ID/CT CHEST ENHANCED                                        
 
         segmed_DATE_TIMEsegmed_DATE_TIME 
          
         Time of examination: segmed_DATE_TIME segmed_DATE_TIME. 
          
         History: Sob . 
          
         Patient Age: 63 years. 
          
         Exam:  CT of chest with contrast: 
          
         Technique: Axial images were obtained after IV contrast, 
         supplemented with sagittal, coronal, and oblique reconstructions: 
          
         Comparison: segmed_DATE_TIME CT chest 
          
         Findings: The pulmonary arteries are well opacified with contrast.   
         No pulmonary emboli are seen.   
         Compared to the prior CT, there has been significant scarring in the 
         right lung with retraction of the heart and mediastinum to the right 
         and elevation of the right hemidiaphragm.  The mass density present 
         in the medial portion of the right upper lobe which previously 
         measured approximately 2 cm in  
         diameter now measures about 3 cm in diameter and is adherent to the 
         right side of the mediastinum.  There is now increased pleural 
         thickening in the right hemithorax posteriorly and slight shift of 
         the superior mediastinum to the right.  Right peritracheal lymph 
         node masses appear stable to  
         slightly decreased in size.  A small superior mediastinal lymph node 
         previously measured about 9 mm and now measures about 11 mm in 
         diameter.  See image 21 of series 4. 
         In the right lower lobe, but there now appears to be endobronchial 
         mucous plugging that was not present previously. 
          
         There are also non-nodular infiltrates in the left lower lobe that 
         are suspicious for metastatic disease. 
         The heart is normal in size. 
         The aorta shows mild calcification in the arch. 
         There is no aortic aneurysm or dissection.   
         Osseous structures show new compression fractures of T9 and T3 that 
 
         PAGE 1               Signed Report                     (CONTINUED)
 
                                          Name: segmed_LASTNAME, segmed_FIRSTNAME             
                                          Phone: segmed_PHONE_NUMBER
                                          Phys: segmed_LASTNAME, segmed_FIRSTNAME E

       Patient Name: segmed_PATIENT_NAME
            Unit No: segmed_ACC_NUM
 
 
EXAM#     TYPE/EXAM                       
segmed_MRN_ID/CT CHEST ENHANCED                                        
        
 
         were not present previously, likely due to metastatic disease. The 
         the degree of compression of T9 is moderate and at T3 is mild.  The 
         slight decrease in height of T4 is unchanged. 
          
         Significant change is that of a new 3.2-cm right adrenal mass that 
         was not present on the prior study, consistent with metastasis.  16 
         mm splenic cyst is unchanged. 
          
         Impression: 1.  Post radiation fibrosis in the right upper lobe with 
         retraction of the heart and mediastinum to the right and elevation 
         of right hemidiaphragm compared to prior CT. 
          
         2. Increase in size of the right upper lobe neoplasm compared to 
         segmed_DATE_TIME. 
          
         3.  New 3-cm right adrenal metastasis. 
          
         4.  New compression fractures at T9 and T3, probably pathologic. 
          
         5.  Stable to slightly decreased mid mediastinal distal right 
         paratracheal adenopathy. 
          
         6.  Increase in size of right paratracheal adenopathy in severe 
         mediastinum. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
         PAGE 2               Signed Report                     (CONTINUED)
